text:
Once upon a time in a quiet village, there lived an enchanted forest. Trees whispered secrets, flowers sang melodies at night, and mysterious glowing fungi would occasionally appear to bathe guests in moonlight.

In the middle of this grove lived a wise old owl named Old Willow. His feathers were as wise as a hundred autumn leaves. Every evening he'd fly gracefully from branch to branch, recounting various woodland tales.

One story Old Willow beloved was about a daring squirrel named Bartholomew. Bartholomew lived high up in an ancient oak tree and loved nothing more than a good hoard of acorn caches.

One autumn, while gathering acorns, Bartholomew discovered a peculiar stone orb. It pulsed with an otherworldly glow. Feeling a pull toward it, Bartholomew slipped out of his cozy nest and climbed up the trunk to examine it.

As he stared into its swirling eyes, Bartholomew imagined himself riding a wind-driven ship through star
